commit.message:
fix(ci): default npm test to local project, use aspire start for CI

Root cause of CI hang: npm test ran all Playwright projects including
production, which hit agents.alwayson.actor (broken SSE) and timed out.

Fixes:
- package.json: 'test' runs --project=local only. Added test:production
  and test:all scripts for explicit use.
- CI: use 'aspire start' (CI-recommended) instead of 'aspire run --detach'.
  No DcpPublisher__RandomizePorts needed — fully dynamic ports.
- Add 'aspire logs e2e' on failure for diagnostics.
- Add 'aspire stop' in cleanup (always runs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): use vanilla dotnet tool install for Aspire CLI, run Playwright directly

The aspire resource e2e approach hangs in CI (DCP process doesn't exit).
Revert to simpler approach:
- dotnet tool install -g Aspire.Cli + GITHUB_PATH
- aspire run --detach + aspire wait (for service discovery)
- npx playwright test --project=local (run directly, not via aspire resource)
- DcpPublisher__RandomizePorts=false in CI for fixed ports

Keeps verify-deployment and production-smoke stages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): Aspire CLI setup, Flux verification, production smoke tests

Three CI improvements:

1. Fix Aspire CLI: use timheuer/setup-aspire action (handles install,
   PATH, caching). Add ASPIRE_ALLOW_UNSECURED_TRANSPORT to all steps.

2. Flux deployment verification (30-min timeout): polls kubectl rollout
   status with retry. On failure collects: pod status, describe, logs
   (current + previous), K8s error events, Flux kustomization/git/image
   policy status. All diagnostics in CI step summary.

3. Production smoke tests: Playwright projects (local/production) with
   @smoke tag. 14 production-safe tests including health, layout, CRUD,
   agent creation, discussion with real LLM, SSE delivery. Runs against
   agents.alwayson.actor after Flux verification succeeds.

Pipeline: build+test → e2e(local) → docker-push → verify-flux → smoke(prod)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: use Aspire CLI for E2E — fully dynamic ports, no hardcoding

Replace manual port polling and DcpPublisher__RandomizePorts=false
with the Aspire CLI's native resource orchestration:
- aspire run --detach: starts AppHost in background
- aspire wait api/web: waits for health with dynamic port discovery
- aspire resource e2e start: launches Playwright with auto-injected
  services__web__http__0 and services__api__http__0 URLs

Added E2E resource to AppHost.Local with .WithReference(api) so
Playwright tests get both web and API URLs injected automatically.

Removed DcpPublisher__RandomizePorts=false from both launchSettings.
All 26 Playwright tests pass with fully dynamic ports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix unhandled promise rejection in InsertNewPageDialog.openDialog() (#776)

After PR #774 modified `page-chat-panel.ts`, the frontend CI exited with
code 1 despite all extension tests passing — because `web-test-runner`
was reporting a failing assertion in `page-chat-panel.test.ts` (tests
still expected the old `error`-setting behavior, not the new
`pendingMessage` queuing behavior). That test fix landed in PR #773.

This PR addresses a second issue visible in CI logs from both the
failing and now-passing runs: an unhandled `TypeError` fired from a
fire-and-forget `.then()` in `openDialog()`.

## Root cause

`automagic-identifier-input` is only type-imported in
`insert-new-page-dialog.ts`, so in tests the custom element is never
upgraded. The shadow DOM query returns an unupgraded `HTMLElement`,
which has no `focusTitleInput` method. The existing `?.` guard only
protected against `null`/`undefined` — not against the property being
`undefined` on a live element:

```typescript
// Before — throws TypeError if element not upgraded
this.updateComplete.then(() => {
  const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
  identifierInput?.focusTitleInput();  // 💥 not a function
});
```

## Fix

Replace the fire-and-forget `.then()` with `await this.updateComplete`
inside the `async` method, and use optional-call syntax (`?.()`) to
defensively handle the unupgraded case:

```typescript
// After — awaited, no unhandled rejection, safe if not upgraded
await this.updateComplete;
const identifierInput = this.shadowRoot?.querySelector<AutomagicIdentifierInput>('automagic-identifier-input');
identifierInput?.focusTitleInput?.();
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: brendanjerwin <3039+brendanjerwin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: auto-clean stale registry entries — define errors out of existence

Revert GetStateAsync/GetInfoAsync/GetPersonaAsync from returning null
back to throwing InvalidOperationException for uninitialized grains.
'Not initialized' IS exceptional — it means the registry is stale.

List endpoints now auto-clean the registry when they discover a stale
entry: log a warning and call UnregisterAsync. This makes the error
impossible on the next call — truly defining it out of existence per
Ousterhout's philosophy.

GET /api/groups/{id} and GET /api/agents/{id} catch
InvalidOperationException at the HTTP boundary → 404. This is the
HTTP layer's job: translate domain exceptions to status codes.

No null, no Result<T>, no silent skipping. Stale entries self-heal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/system file watcher (#3)

* feat: add system file watcher

* ci: add clippy

* ai: Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: mishaszu <mikex@tuta.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: define errors out of existence, remove silent catches

Apply Ousterhout's 'A Philosophy of Software Design' error philosophy:

Define out of existence (no exception, no catch needed):
- GetStateAsync/GetInfoAsync/GetPersonaAsync return null for
  uninitialized grains instead of throwing InvalidOperationException
- Endpoints use null-check instead of try/catch for 404s
- List endpoints filter nulls instead of catching exceptions
- ChatGroupGrain.DeleteAsync and AgentGrain.DeleteAsync drop try/catch
  (orphaned subscriptions are harmless)

Loud errors (log + bubble up):
- LlmIntentGrain: log + rethrow (was silently swallowing LLM failures)
- AgentGrain .Ignore() → .ContinueWith(LogError) for intent grain calls
- Frontend: console.warn on SSE parse failure, console.error on fetch
  failures, console.debug for expected 'group deleted' case

Removed:
- Dead PublishToGroupStream method in OrchestratorService
- All 'catch { /* skip stale entries */ }' patterns
- All silent .catch(() => null) in frontend

11 backend + 26 E2E tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: AppHost.Local LLM fallback + add 5 SSE Playwright tests

AppHost.Local was unconditionally setting OPENAI_ENDPOINT to
localhost:1234 (LM Studio). When LM Studio wasn't running, the
LlmIntentGrain would hang/fail silently, breaking the entire
agent response pipeline. Now only sets the endpoint if explicitly
configured — otherwise falls back to NoOpChatClient.

Added 5 new SSE-specific Playwright tests that prove the full
stream pipeline works end-to-end:
- Message sent via raw API appears in watching browser via SSE
- Agent join event appears as system message via SSE
- Agent leave event appears as system message via SSE
- Discussion responses arrive via SSE in single tab
- Multiple API messages arrive in order via SSE

All 26 Playwright + 11 backend tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: always-on-v2
repository.description: AlwaysOn v2 – globally distributed, mission-critical application examples on Azure

commit.message:
Fix #740: Fix S6551: object stringification issues in TypeScript files (#754)

Fixes all S6551 SonarCloud violations where objects were implicitly or
unsafely stringified in TypeScript UI components.

## Changes Made

### `augment-error-service.ts`
- Replaced `(error as object).toString()` with `String(error)` in the
JSON.stringify catch fallback
- Fixed potential `undefined` message: `JSON.stringify` silently returns
`undefined` for functions and symbols (without throwing), which would
produce `new Error(undefined)`. Fixed with `JSON.stringify(error) ??
String(error)`
- Updated stale comment from "fall back to toString()" to "fall back to
String(...) coercion to preserve a readable representation when
possible"

### `qr-scanner.ts`
- Replaced direct `Object.prototype.toString.call(err)` interpolation in
template literal with a pre-built string variable (S6551-compliant)
- Restored the `else if (typeof err === 'object')` branch with richer
diagnostics: attempts `JSON.stringify` to include object properties
(e.g. `[object Object] {"code":123,"reason":"custom object"}`), falling
back to the type tag if serialization fails

## Testing

- Added tests for the function-value edge case in
`augment-error-service.test.ts` (where `JSON.stringify` returns
`undefined`)
- Updated circular-reference test description to accurately reflect
`String()` coercion
- Updated `qr-scanner.test.ts` to assert that JSON content is included
in the object-error diagnostic message
- All frontend tests pass with 0 ESLint warnings

---------

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Brendan Erwin <brendanjerwin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add score breakdown modal for gameweek transparency

Add an FPL-style score breakdown modal that explains how each golfer's
weekly score was calculated. Users can click on a golfer's week points
in the team table to see a per-tournament breakdown showing:

- Position (1st/2nd/3rd) and base points earned
- Raw score (stableford/medal) and bonus points
- Tournament multiplier applied (2x Weekend Medal, 3x Presidents Cup, etc.)
- Captain multiplier (2x) when applicable
- Grand total for the gameweek

Backend changes:
- Enrich TournamentScoreInfo with tournamentType, scoringFormat, and
  multiplier fields (data was already available, just not exposed)
- Update both team.service.ts and user-profile.ts score formatting

Frontend changes:
- New ScoreBreakdownModal component with responsive design
- TeamGolferTable: week points are now clickable (dotted underline affordance)
- Wire weekLabel prop through from both MyTeamPage and UserProfilePage
- Import shared TournamentScore type in UserProfilePage (replaces local copy)

No new API endpoints  all data was already in the response.needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: show maintain panel only when status is active and improve language detection precision

- Show 'Mantenimento rapporto' section only when that status is selected
- Improve language detection from inbound emails by:
  - stripping quoted/forwarded history markers
  - weighting richer IT/EN lexicons and phrase signals
  - requiring stronger score separation before assigning a flag
  - returning null on ambiguous/low-signal messages

commit.message:
fix: remove sticky positioning from contact detail panel to eliminate scroll

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rename: sub-status 'Risposta ricevuta' → 'Azione richiesta'

Macro group 'Risposta ricevuta' stays as-is (counts all replied contacts).
Sub-status renamed to 'Azione richiesta' = replied but user hasn't responded.
Removed unnecessary .filter() from detail panel status buttons.
DB already migrated (109 contacts).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove vertical scroll from right contact detail panel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make contact detail panel sticky with internal scroll like sidebar

Both panels now stay within viewport bounds. Page-level scrolling eliminated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: separate 'Mantenimento rapporto' from 'Risposta ricevuta' macro group

- Remove 'Mantenimento rapporto' from STATUS_GROUPS['Risposta ricevuta']
- Add standalone pill in header stats for Mantenimento rapporto count
- Add standalone teal filter button in sidebar
- Clean up dead isMantRapporto variable from status buttons
- Mantenimento rapporto now has its own filter, not grouped under Risposta ricevuta

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix scroll jump when clicking status buttons

Save and restore window.scrollY around state updates that
cause sidebar/detail re-renders (status buttons, follow-up
actions). Prevents browser from snapping to top.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement sharing system and update Azure deployment checklist (#53)

* feat: update Azure deployment checklist to reflect completed tasks

* feat: refine sharing system user story with design decisions and implementation phases

* Implement sharing system for accounts and budget plans

- Added domain models for sharing, including AccountShare and BudgetPlanShare with permission levels and share statuses.
- Developed service layer methods for sharing, accepting, declining, and revoking shares.
- Updated repositories to enforce sharing rules and cascade access to related entities.
- Created API endpoints for sharing operations and integrated them into the existing controllers.
- Developed Blazor UI components for sharing functionality, including modals and shared resources display.
- Implemented end-to-end tests to validate sharing behavior and integration tests for service logic.
- Added unit tests to ensure correct handling of sharing scenarios and permission checks.

* refactor: streamline user story refinement recommendations and update status overview

* feat: add workflow to keep Supabase alive with scheduled pings

* feat: update authentication service to return detailed responses for email actions

* chore: update workflow configuration for Supabase ping job

* refactor: simplify logging messages for account and budget plan sharing

* refactor: simplify logging messages for account and budget plan share actions

* refactor: improve error messages for user not found in account and budget plan sharing

* feat: add checks for existing account and budget plan shares before sharing

* feat: prevent users from sharing accounts and budget plans with themselves

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace Timer with CancellationTokenSource for periodic refresh in NavMenu

* feat: restrict account editing and sharing to account owners

* feat: implement user permission checks for budget plan modifications and sharing

* feat: add error handling and logging for sharing operations in SharingController

* feat: enhance permission checks for budget plans and lines, adding logging for unauthorized access attempts

* feat: simplify account access checks by introducing GetSharedAccessibleAccountIdsQuery method

* feat: add archiving checks to sharing operations to prevent interactions with archived shares

* feat: add data-testid attribute to pending share badge for improved testability

* feat: update pending share badge for improved readability and add new tests for budget plan sharing functionality

* feat: enhance account retrieval to include shared accounts accepted by the user

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: inject IUserContext to manage user-specific data in Accounts page

* feat: validate and normalize target email in ShareBudgetPlanAsync method

* feat: include check for archived shares in GetAllActiveAsync method

* Update LocalFinanceManager/Data/Repositories/BudgetLineRepository.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: include archived shares check in repositories and services for accurate data retrieval

* fix: apply review feedback - auth messages, nav logging, account ownership, editor permissions

Fix: 'Mantenere in contatto' now sets status to 'Mantenimento rapporto' and clears follow-up

- enableKeepInTouch: sets status to 'Mantenimento rapporto', clears next_action_at/note
- followUpSummary: excludes contacts with 'Mantenimento rapporto' status
- Fixed Joshua Iannello and Matteo Paba in DB
